title: Trinamool Congress candidate for Falta Assembly seat Jahangir Khan withdraws from contest
content: Party condemns move, states decision to withdraw from the Falta re-poll is Jahangir Khan’s personal choice and not that of the party.

title: Study reveals complex mating behaviour of Indian squid, documents evidence of same-sex mating
content: Researchers found sperm carrying structures (spermatangia) implanted on the buccal membrane of a male squid, providing the reportedly first documented evidence of same-sex sexual behaviour of Indian squid

title: All major pharmacy chains, hospital-based medical stores, Jan Aushadhi Kendras to remain open on May 20
content: Several State-level pharmacies have opted out of the proposed nationwide shutdown on May 20, committing to ensure there will be no disruption in the availability of medicines to the public

title: Philanthropy Asia Alliance mobilised $615 million to support over 300 projects in climate action, healthcare, inclusive growth
content: The Rockefeller Foundation and Temasek Trust on Tuesday announced the launch of the Global Coalition for Nuclear Philanthropy to support the safe expansion of nuclear energy.

title: Court denies pre-arrest bail to Bhopal lawyer wanted for wife's death; cops declare bounty for info
content: During the tense courtroom hearing, Samarth’s counsel opposed arrest by raising questions about Twisha’s mental health, a statement that was strongly countered by the deceased woman’s family lawyer

title: Chikkamagaluru DC transferred within four-and-a-half months
content: Speculation is rife that the transfer is linked to recent developments surrounding the recounting of postal ballots in the Sringeri constituency

title: Heavy discounts, old prescriptions and 10-minute deliveries: Why thousands of pharmacies in Telangana are shutting down on May 20
content: The shutdown call was given by the All India Organisation of Chemists and Druggists (AIOCD), raising concerns over unfair practices by online pharmacies and corporate medicine retail chains

title: Stage set for revision of land and registration values across the State
content: The enhancement is not uniform and is based on different slabs for different categories of lands and houses/apartments; new rates likely to be effective from May 28, say officials

title: Kerala’s UDF govt. forms expert panel to prepare White Paper on State finances
content: The committee will be headed by former Vice-Chairperson of Kerala State Planning Board K.M. Chandrasekhar. Additional Chief Secretary (Finance) K.R. Jyothilal has been appointed as convener

title: Rahul Gandhi takes dig at PM Modi on Norway pension fund dropping Adani shares from portfolio
content: In February 2026, Norway’s $1.2 trillion sovereign wealth fund, the world's largest, decided to exclude Adani Green Energy Ltd. from its portfolio due to concern about its alleged links to financial crime

title: BITS Pilani Hyderabad researchers develop graphene electrode for improved supercapacitor performance
content: The work was carried out by scientists at the MEMS, Microfluidics and Nanoelectronics (MMNE) Lab and published recently in the journal Surfaces and Interfaces

title: Will remain committed to Tamil Nadu’s two-language policy in schools: TVK Minister Rajmohan
content: Mr. Rajmohan’s comments assume significance as the Union Ministry of Education had recently sent a reminder regarding the implementation of the Pradhan Mantri Schools for Rising India (PM SHRI) scheme in Tamil Nadu.

title: Modi in Norway LIVE: PM Modi holds bilateral talks with  Finland PM Petteri Orpo
content: President of the European Commission Ursula von der Leyen once again called for an investment agreement between India and the European Union (EU) as India-EU free trade agreement will be discussed again in the summit

title: India still short on expertise, tools to manage fungal health burden
content: The good news is researchers in India are working to identify priority fungal pathogens circulating within its borders, both including and beyond a list of species the WHO released in 2022. They are also working to map antifungal resistance in pathogens in the environment and develop newer therapeutics such as antimicrobial peptides

title: Telangana: Two killed in a freak accident while arranging concrete rings in agricultural well
content: The incident occurred when a large mass of earth caved in while the labourers were placing concrete rings along the periphery of the well

title: Women-led drone patrol foils theft; arrests repeat offender at Yakutpura railway station
content: Since the launch of the ‘Pink Patrol’ last month, RPF has registered 388 cases related to unauthorised entry into ladies’ coaches, along with 69 cases against trespassers and suspicious individuals on railway premises.

title: Over 100 labourers from four Southern States rescued as massive raids continue at Nizamabad brick kilns
content: Workers, including children, were allegedly kept in bondage-like conditions; police say criminal cases will be filed after rescue operation concludes

title: Telangana DCA assures uninterrupted supply of essential medicines during May 20 shutdown
content: According to the advisory, a meeting was held with the Telangana chapter of the AIOCD, during which it was decided that pharmacy stores attached to hospitals would remain open to avoid inconvenience to patients and the publi

title: Maoism eradicated from country before March 31 deadline due to valour of forces: Amit Shah
content: Amit Shah said that he could say this without any hesitation that many non-BJP governments in different States have helped the Union government in its plans to eliminate Maoism, but the previous Congress government in Chhattisgarh did not provide help

title: Telangana reels under scorching heat, 45℃ mark breached in four Districts
content: Kotgiri in Nizamabad district emerged as the hottest location in the State, recording a maximum temperature of 45.6℃

title: Kochi P&T Apartment owners issue ultimatum threatening to lay siege to GCDA office over persistent leakage
content: Residents have been protesting against the persistent leakage since 78 families moved in in January 2024. They have also raised concerns about cracks and an alleged tilt in the buildings

title: V.D. Satheesan sworn in as the new Chief Minister of Kerala
content: Governor Rajendra Vishwanath Arlekar administered the oath of office and secrecy to Mr. Satheesan and the Ministers-designate over a one-hour ceremony

title: Near-vision glasses improved productivity among Bengaluru garment workers: Study
content: Researchers found that workers who received near-vision glasses recorded a 5.7% to 6% increase in productivity over a 12-week period compared with those who did not receive immediate vision correction.

title: Youth Congress leaders, workers fight amongst themselves at KPCC office
content: A section of Youth Congress workers and leaders — upset over the recent suspension of 17 people — were incensed at the action taken by State Youth Congress president H.N. Manjunath Gowda, condemning his “dictatorial” attitude

title: A.P. TDP chief gives up Y-category security convoy after PM’s fuel appeal
content: Telugu Desam Party State chief Palla Srinivasa Rao returns bullet-proof vehicle, drops green-channel movement, says public representatives must lead by example

title: Milk-pouring ritual in Narmada: NGT seeks response from pollution control boards
content: Nearly 11,000 litres of milk were poured into the river as part of a ritual on the culmination of a 21-day religious event

title: Twins Jinil Rexa and Jivin Rexa on finding their space in Malayalam cinema as actors
content: The twins are in the limelight post the success of Mohiniyattam, the sequel of the 2024-film Bharathanatyam

title: At the Congress’ convention, Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah attacks BJP and PM Narendra Modi
content: The attack on BJP by CM Siddaramaiah looked to counter PM Modi who earlier, during his visit to Bengaluru, had ridiculed the State Government and attacked the Congress.

title: Supreme Court collegium recommends 19 persons for appointment as judges of Madras High Court
content: The incumbent Registrar General of the High Court S. Alli is among those whose names have been recommended for elevation

title: Delhi Police seeks larger Bench reference on UAPA bail curbs amid ‘conflicting’ coordinate Bench rulings
content: Additional Solicitor General S.V. Raju said the apex court’s May 18 ruling overlooked that under the UAPA’s statutory bail bar, the presumption of innocence takes a ‘backseat’

title: Greater Noida dowry death: Postmortem reveals rupture of internal organs
content: According to the postmortem report of Deepika Nagar, bleeding was present from the victim’s left nostril and mouth

title: SP-Congress alliance will continue, focus will be on victory not seats: Akhilesh Yadav
content: Akhilesh Yadav said that future alliance discussions would not revolve around bargaining for seats but around ensuring electoral success

title: Bail is the rule is not an empty slogan, even in UAPA and ‘terror’ cases: Supreme Court
content: The court says the threshold for denying bail under the UAPA is so low that the state has only to show that the accusations were ‘prima facie’ true. The trial may continue for years and pre-trial incarceration begins to acquire a post-trial punitive character, it points out

title: After Netherlands, MEA faces questions over PM’s decision not to hold press conference in Norway
content: MEA Secretary (West) Sibi George defends policy, says India’s constitution guarantees press freedoms, democracy and human rights

title: Steps into past: 1,500-year-old reservoir unearthed on Elephanta island
content: Archaeological Survey of India excavation finds sophisticated stepped reservoir and proof of ancient maritime trade

title: Restaurant owner arrested in Madurai for trafficking Indian nationals to cyber scam compounds in Cambodia
content: The accused, identified as Madhan Vadivel, received commissions for each recruitment. He even ran restaurants inside the compounds in Cambodia and supplied Indian food to the victims

title: Return of Leiden copper plates should spark efforts for further repatriations, say Indian archaeologists
content: Efforts needed to bring back copper plates issued by an 8th-century Pandya ruler from the British Museum; inscriptions on Leiden copper plates record a Chola gift of land to build a Buddhist vihara

title: Malayali youngsters develop rail hygiene kit addressing sanitary concerns over long distance train travel
content: Rail hygiene kit by Travel Essentia, a startup founded by four Malayali youngsters, consists of 10 components for maintaining personal hygiene

title: Bengaluru, now book an appointment for bulky waste to be picked up from your doorstep
content: The Bengaluru Solid Waste Management Limited (BSWML), responsible for Solid Waste Management in the city, has launched a new mobile app ‘DClutter’ through which you can book for such a visit by waste collectors

title: CBSE 2026 re-evaluation: How to apply for revision of marks?
content: The results for the highly awaited exams were declared on May 13, 2026, with a dip in pass percentage being reported.

title: Vizag autorickshaw drivers protest fuel price hike, demand subsidised rates from State government
content: CITU-affiliated union marches from Jagadamba junction to a petrol bunk, says drivers cannot earn even ₹300 a day after free bus scheme for women

title: European Union prepares to cut U.S. import duties to avoid Trump tariff hike
content: President Donald Trump has said he would impose much higher tariffs on EU goods if the European Union did not implement its trade ‌deal commitments by July 4

title: Over 5,000 metro piers and portals to get advertisements across Bengaluru
content: BMRCL plans to introduce both static and digital advertisement panels on Metro piers and portals, while ensuring that the displays are aesthetically designed and comply with advertisement regulations.

title: Tamil Nadu government to establish Talent Research Wing for meritorious sportspersons: Minister Aadhav Arjuna
content: The Minister instructed officials to ensure all basic needs of sportspersons were promptly addressed.

title: Supreme Court extends tenure of all retiring tribunal heads, members till September 8
content: A Bench comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M Pancholi passed the order during the mentioning of a pending case

title: Operation Sindoor reflected ‘smart power’ in its most complete expression, says Army Chief Dwivedi
content: In his address at a seminar hosted at the Manekshaw Centre in New Delhi, Gen Upendra Dwivedi also said that twelve months ago, India offered the world a "partial answer" to the so-called smart power question

title: Andhra Pradesh bans bulk fuel sales at 4,500 outlets, caps container fills at 200 litres
content: Civil Supplies Commissioner directs District Collectors to ensure petrol pumps fill fuel directly into vehicle tanks; 200-litre cap on container fills, no bulk supply

title: ‘Economic storm’ ahead; youth and small traders to be worst hit: Rahul Gandhi in Raebareli
content: The Congress MP, who is on two-day visit, also accused the prime minister of not following his own call for austerity.

title: Why has the RSS called for dialogue with Pakistan?
content: What did RSS leader Dattatreya Hosabale say about ties with Pakistan and what reactions did his remarks trigger in India and Pakistan? Can his statement be read as reflecting government policy? What has the RSS’s stance on talks with Pakistan been?

title: Name a country that does not use nuke power for energy needs, asks Supreme Court
content: Petitioner confronts SHANTI Act with 40-year-old Oleum gas leak case verdict which made pvt entities engaged in hazardous activity absolutely liable to community in case of an accident

title: 25-Year-Old Odisha Man Killed In Ukranian Drone Attack In Russia
content: The victim had gone to Russia in March reportedly through a private firm Micon Agency. He joined the compnay in April along with seven other people from his state.

title: 7-Year-Old Boy Electrocuted After Live Wire Falls On Road In Karnataka
content: A CCTV video of the incident has surfaced, capturing the moment of the electrocution. Visuals showed the child collapsing after coming into contact with the live wire, after which people present at the spot dragged his body away.

title: "She Said Dad If Things Don't Work Out...": Twisha Sharma's Parents Break Down
content: Twisha Sharma's father claimed that her 33-year-old daughter was being subjected to various forms of abuse, including physical abuse.

title: Video: Released On Bail, Rape-Accused Garlanded, Carried On Shoulders In UP
content: Soon after his release, the rape-accused, dressed in white and covered in marigold garlands, held a roadshow, triggering outrage.

title: Karnataka Woman Dies By Suicide, Alleges Dowry Harassment By Vet Husband
content: Aishwarya's death was reported from Kampli town, days after a similar case of the death of a newly married woman, Twisha Sharma, left the nation in shock.

title: Thailand Cuts Visa-Free Stays For Tourists, Citing Crime By Foreigners
content: Thailand is drastically cutting the length of visa-free stays for tourists from more than 90 countries in an effort to curb crime involving foreign nationals, officials said on Tuesday.

title: Rules Eased, US Eyes India's New $300-Billion Nuclear Goldmine
content: US nuclear companies happy with amendments in the Indian nuclear liability law, but want industry-friendly rules.

title: Vijay Pays Tribute To Ex-LTTE Chief, BJP Targets Rahul Gandhi, TVK Responds
content: Vijay's tribute for the former LTTE chief is rare since parties in power in Tamil Nadu generally refrain from being seen in support of Prabhakaran.

title: Twisha Sharma "Needed Continuous Monitoring": Mother-In-Law To NDTV
content: Twisha Sharma would say "don't monitor me" and "don't force me" every time she was asked to take her medicines, said her mother-in-law Giribala Singh.

title: Opinion: Opinion | 3 Blasts In A Week: Pakistan's Taliban Blowback Is Entering A Dangerous New Phase
content: Last week, multiple terror attacks rocked the restive provinces of Balochistan and Khyber Pakhtunkhwa. First came an IED attack, then a suicide blast, with militants taking weapons and police personnel from the site, then a bomb blast.

title: PM Modi Holds Talks With Nordic Counterparts Ahead Of Oslo Summit
content: The Nordic countries refer to a geopolitical and cultural region in Northern Europe and the North Atlantic. It comprises Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway and Sweden.

title: Trinamool's Jahangir Khan Pulls Out Of Falta Contest 2 Days Before Repoll
content: Trinamool spokesperson Arup Chakraborty said the party has received information about Jahangir Khan's decision to withdraw from Falta bypoll, but is yet to get clarity on what prompted it.

title: Rehman Gali Becomes Ram Gali: Why Pak Is Changing Names Of Lahore Streets
content: History bears witness that, ever since the partition, the plight of minorities in Pakistan-- particularly Hindus and Sikhs -- has been a matter of grave concern. However, the name change drive has unfolded with almost no organised opposition in a cou

title: US Iran War News Live Updates: Trump Holds Off Iran Attack Planned For Tuesday After Gulf Intervention
content: US Iran War News LIVE Updates: Israel launched a massive series of airstrikes on southern Lebanon, despite an extension of the truce between the two countries.

title: Garlands, roadshow: Rape accused gets rousing welcome in UP after bail - video
content: An alleged rape accused in Ghaziabad received a triumphant welcome from supporters upon his bail, with videos showing him being carried on shoulders amidst cheers and a procession. Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav criticized the state government, calling the reception reprehensible and highlighting the perceived hypocrisy.

title: 'CM Adhikari promised ...': Jahangir Khan exits Falta repoll; TMC says 'succumbed to pressure'
content: Trinamool Congress candidate Jahangir Khan has withdrawn his nomination for the Falta re-poll, days before voting. The Election Commission had previously scrapped the original polls due to severe electoral offenses and subversion of the democratic process. Observer reports highlighted significant irregularities, including EVM tampering and voter intimidation. This development follows criticism from West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari.

title: Bumrah, Jadeja rested for AFG series as Prince Yadav earns maiden call-up
content: India have announced squads for the Afghanistan one-off Test and ODI series, with Ravindra Jadeja rested from the series. Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli feature in the ODI squad, though Rohit and Hardik Pandya’s participation remains subject to fitness clearance. Shubman Gill will lead India in both formats, while Gurnoor Brar, Harsh Dubey and Prince Yadav are among the notable inclusions.

title: Meta layoffs: Employees told to work from home as countdown begins for 4 am
content: Meta is laying off around 8,000 employees on Wednesday, May 20, with notification emails going out in three regional waves starting at 4 AM local time. A leaked memo from HR chief Janelle Gale tells North American staff to work from home as the cuts roll out. Meanwhile, 7,000 workers are being reassigned to four new AI-focused organisations as Mark Zuckerberg restructures Meta around flatter teams, fewer managers, and a $145 billion AI bet.

title: Builder hasn't delivered your flat? What RERA says and how to file a complaint
content: Homebuyers facing delayed possession or unfair practices by builders now have legal recourse under RERA. The Act empowers buyers to seek compensation for delays or opt for a full refund with interest.  It's crucial to file complaints early with supporting documents to protect your rights and investment.

title: Before Tejas, there was Marut: India’s first supersonic fighter jet & lessons it offers
content: India's ambitious Marut supersonic fighter, designed by Kurt Tank, faced early setbacks due to underfunded engines, limiting its performance. Despite this, it proved effective in the 1971 war, destroying enemy tanks. Bureaucratic indecision ultimately doomed upgrades, mirroring current challenges with the Tejas program's engine development and integration, highlighting a recurring theme in India's aviation history.

title: Anthropic CEO makes a 'doomsday' prediction for software companies
content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei told WSJ at the World Economic Forum in Davos that software is heading toward being "cheap, maybe essentially free," warning that whole careers built around writing code may not survive the AI shift. The Anthropic chief executive also predicted SaaS incumbents leaning on code complexity as a moat could "go bust" as ServiceNow, Snowflake and Microsoft stocks slide and Anthropic eyes a $900 billion valuation.

title: Punjabi singer Yashinder Kaur found dead days after being kidnapped at gunpoint in Ludhiana
content: A Punjabi singer, Inder Kaur, was found dead in Neelon Canal five days after her alleged kidnapping at gunpoint. Her car, found nearby with blood stains, suggests she was murdered before being dumped. Police have arrested the father and friend of her former boyfriend, Sukhwinder Singh, who recently returned from Canada.

title: Language-based AI systems could aid in organising archaeology reports
content: A new book explores Indian archaeology's post-independence journey, highlighting the decolonization challenges faced by the ASI. It emphasizes Amalananda Ghosh's impactful leadership and discusses the ongoing issues of training and employment for archaeologists. The article also touches upon the potential of AI in aiding archaeological research and data management.

title: India, UAE forge deep defence alliance: $5bn investment, joint production of drones & missiles
content: India and the UAE have significantly deepened their defence ties in 2026, moving towards joint industrial production of advanced weaponry and enhanced maritime and cyber security. This strategic partnership, backed by substantial UAE investment, aims to bolster India's defence manufacturing and secure vital trade routes amidst regional instability.

title: French AI startup Mistral CEO gives Europe two years to 'fight' American companies
content: Europe faces a critical two-year deadline to build its own AI infrastructure. Mistral AI's Arthur Mensch warns of US technology dependence if Europe fails to act. Control over chips, energy, and computing power is key to AI competition. Failure could leave Europe reliant on American digital services. Mistral is working to expand Europe's AI capacity.

title: What RR, PBKS, KKR, DC and CSK need to do to grab the final IPL 2026 playoff spot
content: The race for the final IPL playoff spot intensifies with five teams vying for one berth. Rajasthan Royals are best positioned, needing just one win, while Punjab Kings control their destiny. Kolkata Knight Riders, Delhi Capitals, and Chennai Super Kings require victories and favorable results elsewhere. Every remaining match significantly impacts the playoff picture.

title: Putin to visit India for Brics summit in September
content: Russian President Vladimir Putin will attend the BRICS summit in New Delhi on September 12-13.  Kremlin aide Yury Ushakov confirmed his participation in the national capital. This visit marks a significant diplomatic event for India amidst global geopolitical shifts and growing BRICS collaboration.  Details on his schedule are anticipated.

title: India’s PNG push hits roadblocks: What’s slowing the pipeline expansion
content: India's ambitious piped natural gas expansion is significantly behind schedule, with city gas distribution companies connecting only a fraction of the government's daily target. Acute shortages of certified gas plumbers and weak consumer uptake, particularly in rental housing, are major impediments to achieving the 2030 goal.

title: 'Deeply concerned: WHO raises red flag over 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
content: A severe Ebola outbreak, caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, has claimed 130 lives with over 500 suspected cases.  The World Health Organization warns of high risk to neighboring nations, stressing human-to-human transmission via bodily fluids and contact with infected animals.  Emergency response systems are being activated to combat the escalating crisis.

title: 'Ladke jhool nai paate, they are treated as criminals': Mother-in-law on Twisha Sharma 'dowry' death
content: A model-actor's suspicious death case in Bhopal intensifies as her husband, a lawyer, is denied anticipatory bail and a reward is offered for his arrest. Allegations of dowry harassment and abetment to suicide are being probed by a Special Investigation Team. Conflicting claims emerge from both families regarding the circumstances leading to her death.

title: Why SC refused to recall its stray dog relocation order: 'Can’t remain oblivious to harsh realities'
content: The Supreme Court dismissed pleas to recall its stray dog relocation and sterilization order, issuing directions to states to augment infrastructure. The court allowed euthanasia for rabid or dangerous stray dogs, emphasizing the need for expert assessment and adherence to animal welfare laws.

title: $25 billion and counting: Iran war burns a hole in corporate balance sheets
content: A US-Israeli war with Iran has triggered significant global economic fallout, with companies reporting at least $25 billion in losses due to soaring oil prices and disrupted trade routes. Businesses worldwide are implementing cost-cutting measures, including price hikes and reduced production, as the conflict impacts various sectors, particularly airlines.

title: How India’s fuel price rise compares to US, China, Pak & other economies
content: Economists say that if the situation does not improve in the coming days, then more hikes may be in the offing. However, the approach of gradual hikes in petrol and diesel prices is to avoid an immediate inflation shock to the economy.

title: 'We are proud democracy': MEA vs Norwegian press during PM Modi's 4th leg of five-nation tour
content: Ministry of external affairs pushed back against press freedom queries during PM Modi's Norway visit, citing a lack of understanding of India's scale and reliance on "ignorant NGOs." This followed a Norwegian journalist's claim that PM Modi avoided her question. The MEA defended India's democratic framework and rights, highlighting its vast population and media landscape.

title: ‘Kya middle stump udaya bey’: Rohit’s funny act leaves MI, KKR in splits | Watch
content: Ahead of a crucial IPL 2026 clash, Rohit Sharma lightened the mood with a hilarious impression of KKR coach Abhishek Nayar during Mumbai Indians' training.  While Rohit's antics brought laughter, Kolkata Knight Riders face immense pressure to win against MI to keep their playoff hopes alive, despite a recent resurgence.

title: BJP MLA’s mobile phone snatched during morning walk in Raipur
content: A senior BJP leader's phone was snatched in Raipur during a morning walk, sparking a political row over law and order. Police swiftly apprehended a 19-year-old suspect and recovered the device within eight hours. The incident drew criticism from the Congress, which questioned the effectiveness of the city's police commissionerate system amidst rising crime rates.

title: Delhi horror: Man sets himself ablaze outside wife’s house amid matrimonial dispute
content: A man tragically died by self-immolation in northwest Delhi's Jahangirpuri following a heated argument related to an ongoing matrimonial dispute. He reportedly threatened suicide before setting himself ablaze outside his wife's home. Police are investigating the precise sequence of events leading to this devastating incident.

title: 'We're also trying to provide for our family': Jhingan as ISL faces another crisis
content: As Indian football faces uncertainty, 148 top-tier players, including captain Sandesh Jhingan, will be out of contract on May 31 with no renewal discussions. With negotiations stalled between clubs and the AIFF over the commercial partner, there's no clear roadmap for next season. Players and staff fear job losses with clubs seriously contemplating closing down.

title: 'Anil Pandit USA’ to ‘Manna Brampton’: Why new-age gangsters prefer foreign suffix
content: North India's notorious gangsters are shedding village-based aliases for international monikers like 'Fateh Portugal' and 'Manna Brampton'. This shift signals a globalized underworld, using foreign locations for intimidation and to evade local law enforcement.  These 'global franchises' now target celebrities and operate with a transnational reach, posing a significant challenge to policing worldwide.

title: India's best-looking Mahindra Scorpios? Check new Indian Air Force SUV fleet
content: The Indian Air Force has received a new fleet of Mahindra Scorpio Classic S11 SUVs, distinguished by a striking Air Force Blue paint job. These rugged, rear-wheel-drive vehicles, powered by a 130 bhp diesel engine, are a familiar sight in defense and government services. Spotted on Delhi roads, these specially finished Scorpios stand out, showcasing their unique military livery.

title: Has Russia lost 10% of its refining capacity? Ukraine launches drones as Moscow attacks Danube port city
content: Ukraine launched drone strikes on Russian regions, including those housing oil infrastructure, while Russia retaliated with attacks on Ukrainian port facilities. President Zelenskyy highlighted a significant drop in Russia's oil refining capacity due to these actions. Efforts to end the protracted conflict remain stalled, with both sides continuing cross-border strikes and accusing each other of targeting vital infrastructure.

title: Historic Big Bash opener in Chennai likely to feature Perth against Sydney
content: Chennai's MA Chidambaram Stadium is set to make history by hosting the opening match of the Big Bash T20 League in December. This landmark event marks the first time a foreign franchise league game will be played on Indian soil. Cricket Australia officials have inspected the venue, signaling a significant step in strengthening India-Australia cricketing ties.

title: SC rejects plea to stay earlier order on stray dogs; cites 'absence of sustained efforts' by states
content: The Supreme Court has refused to stay its order for stray dog removal from public institutions, citing the failure of state governments to implement Animal Birth Control Rules. The court emphasized that the right to life includes living free from the threat of dog bites and directed high courts to monitor compliance.

title: 'Provided Rs 50,000 regularly': Twisha's mother-in-law counters dowry allegations
content: A model's suspicious death has triggered a legal storm in Bhopal, with a court rejecting the anticipatory bail plea of her absconding husband, Samarth Singh. His mother, a retired judge, also accused, was granted bail. The family alleges dowry harassment and murder, claims denied by the accused who cite the deceased's alleged mental health issues.

title: ‘Tn07 Cm 2026’ scene goes viral: Venkat Prabhu gifts GOAT plate to CM Vijay
content: Director Venkat Prabhu presented Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay with the 'TN07 CM 2026' number plate from the film 'The Greatest of All Time'. This gesture comes after a scene from the 2024 movie, featuring Vijay with the same registration, went viral, with fans now viewing it as a political prediction for the 2026 elections.

title: Delhi HC issues contempt notice to Kejriwal, Sisodia over ‘vilifying’ posts against judge
content: Delhi High Court has issued notices to Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia, and other AAP leaders in a criminal contempt case. This action stems from alleged defamatory social media posts targeting a judge after she declined to recuse herself from the Delhi excise policy case. The court noted a campaign to portray the judge as biased, distinct from fair criticism.

title: How soaring crude prices are hitting India: Explained in 10 charts
content: Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East are significantly impacting India's economy. Rising crude oil prices have led to a Rs 3 per litre fuel hike, eroded investor wealth by over Rs 5.85 lakh crore, and pushed the rupee to an all-time low. Foreign investors are also withdrawing funds, while the import bill has surged.

title: ‘My body is little weak’: MS Dhoni’s comment to Raina sparks retirement fears
content: Suresh Raina urged MS Dhoni to play IPL 2027, dismissing concerns about his fitness after CSK's loss.  Fans at Chepauk were emotional, fearing it was Dhoni's last home game.  Despite the defeat, Dhoni's presence and lap of appreciation captivated the crowd, leaving his future uncertain but fueling hope for another season.

title: BJP civic chief offers Rs 1 crore reward for beheading Trinamool MP Saayoni Ghosh
content: A BJP leader in Uttar Pradesh announced a Rs 1 crore reward for beheading a Trinamool Congress MP over an old social media post. The remarks, made during a protest against alleged insult to Hindu deities, have sparked a political row. The MP had previously stated her account was hacked when the post was made.

title: ‘We’re judged on results’: Stephen Fleming breaks silence on his CSK future
content: Chennai Super Kings' playoff hopes are slim after a home defeat, casting a shadow over head coach Stephen Fleming's future. Fleming acknowledged his tenure rests with CSK management, while backing captain Ruturaj Gaikwad's transition. Injuries and a squad overhaul presented challenges, though MS Dhoni's influence remains significant despite not playing.

title: ‘Tough times are coming’: Rahul Gandhi warns of ‘economic storm’ after fuel price hike

title: Twisha Sharma's family seek second autopsy, claims she had pre-death injuries
content: The TOI News Desk comprises a dedicated and tireless team of journalists who operate around the clock to deliver the most current and comprehensive news and updates to the readers of The Times of India worldwide. With an unwavering commitment to excellence in journalism, our team is at the forefront of gathering, verifying, and presenting breaking news, in-depth analysis, and insightful reports on a wide range of topics. The TOI News Desk is your trusted source for staying informed and connected to the ever-evolving global landscape, ensuring that our readers are equipped with the latest developments that matter most."
